components.name: D-Glucose
components.evidence: M9 medium, supplemented with 1 % (w/v) glucose, 100 mM MOPS and 50 mg/L tryptophan, was used for assessing growth of genome-reduced strains [46].

components.name: MOPS (3-(N-morpholino)propanesulfonic acid)
components.evidence: M9 medium, supplemented with 1 % (w/v) glucose, 100 mM MOPS and 50 mg/L tryptophan, was used for assessing growth of genome-reduced strains [46].

components.name: L-Tryptophan
components.evidence: M9 medium, supplemented with 1 % (w/v) glucose, 100 mM MOPS and 50 mg/L tryptophan, was used for assessing growth of genome-reduced strains [46].

components.name: Disodium hydrogen phosphate (Na2HPO4.2H2O)
components.source: cited:Harwood & Cutting 1990 (ref 46; recipe via SubtiWiki M9)
components.evidence: SubtiWiki M9 minimal medium (ref Harwood and Cutting 1990): Na2HPO4.2H2O 8.5 g/L, KH2PO4 3.0 g/L, NH4Cl 1.0 g/L, NaCl 0.5 g/L.

components.source: cited:Harwood & Cutting 1990 (ref 46; recipe via SubtiWiki M9)
components.evidence: SubtiWiki M9 minimal medium (ref Harwood and Cutting 1990): KH2PO4 3.0 g/L.

components.source: cited:Harwood & Cutting 1990 (ref 46; recipe via SubtiWiki M9)
components.evidence: SubtiWiki M9 minimal medium (ref Harwood and Cutting 1990): NH4Cl 1.0 g/L.

components.name: Sodium chloride (NaCl)

components.evidence: SubtiWiki M9 minimal medium (ref Harwood and Cutting 1990): NaCl 0.5 g/L.

components.evidence: SubtiWiki M9: MgSO4 1.0 mM (added separately after autoclaving).

components.name: Ferric chloride (FeCl3, in citric acid)

components.evidence: SubtiWiki M9: FeCl3 0.05 mM (in 0.1 M citric acid).

components.name: Citric acid / citrate
components.concentration: iron-chelate carrier (0.1 M citric acid in Fe stock)
components.source: cited:Harwood & Cutting 1990 (ref 46; recipe via SubtiWiki M9)
components.evidence: SubtiWiki M9: FeCl3 0.05 mM in 0.1 M citric acid.

components.name: Manganese(II) chloride (MnCl2.4H2O)
components.concentration: 1 mg/L final (100 mg/L in 100x trace stock)
components.source: cited:Harwood & Cutting 1990 (ref 46; trace mix via SubtiWiki M9)
components.evidence: SubtiWiki M9 trace elements (100x): MnCl2.4H2O 100 mg, ZnCl2 170 mg, CuCl2.2H2O 43 mg, CoCl2.6H2O 60 mg, Na2MoO4.2H2O 60 mg (per L stock).

components.concentration: 1.7 mg/L final (170 mg/L in 100x trace stock)
components.source: cited:Harwood & Cutting 1990 (ref 46; trace mix via SubtiWiki M9)
components.evidence: SubtiWiki M9 trace elements (100x): ZnCl2 170 mg per L stock.

components.evidence: SubtiWiki M9 trace elements (100x): CuCl2.2H2O 43 mg per L stock.

media.base_medium: M9 minimal medium (Bacillus subtilis formulation per Harwood & Cutting 1990) + 1% (w/v) glucose, 100 mM MOPS, 50 mg/L L-tryptophan

sources_chased.what: paper main text (PMC4893254 Methods) for the exact supplementation over M9
base M9 recipe cited as ref [46] = Harwood CR, Cutting SM. Molecular biological methods for Bacillus. Wiley, 1990
sources_chased.how: reference identified in fullTextXML (CR46); standard B. subtilis M9 recipe recovered from SubtiWiki M9 page, which cites the same Harwood & Cutting 1990 source

media.notes: Organism is Bacillus subtilis (BSF1 and genome-reduced derivatives); cultivation was strictly aerobic shake-flask (37 C, 220-240 rpm, 100 mL in 500 mL flasks), so oxygen is aerobic rather than facultative. Main text gives only the three supplements over M9; the base M9 salts + FeCl3 + full trace-element mix were recovered from the cited Harwood & Cutting 1990 B. subtilis M9 (SubtiWiki formulation, same reference). This M9 contains no vitamins (no thiamine). The only trace element truly absent from the prior component set was molybdenum (Mo); Fe, Mn, Zn, Cu, Co were already present as open exchanges. Trace-element final concentrations assume 1:100 dilution of the 100x stock.
